title: SynapShield: Intercepting Parkinson's Disease at the Gut-Brain Interface
description:
SynapShield is a bioengineered hydrogel system designed to intercept Parkinson's disease at its gut-brain origin - 15-20 years before motor symptoms appear.

By targeting the pyloric/duodenal boundary where enteroendocrine cells interface with the vagus nerve, SynapShield acts as a pathological sink, trapping misfolded alpha--synuclein proteins and releasing neuroprotective agents over a 10-15 year timeline.

Key achievements:

  • ✅ 4-species PDE model (drugs + pathogen)
  • ✅ >94% alpha--synuclein reduction at vagal boundary
  • ✅ 15-year zero-order drug release profile
  • ✅ Shear-thinning injectable biomaterial (Herschel-Bulkley)
  • ✅ FEniCSx poroelastic multiphysics validation
  • ✅ Open-source computational validation

Preprint: medRxiv - submitted June 30, 2026 - DOI pending
Live demo: https://artistso.github.io/synapshield/

Dedicated to Richard, Ocean Shores, WA.

creators.name: Owens, Steven
